user_outcome: RunSlice performs its captain, implement, and verify legs exclusively through registry-resolved Driver.Dispatch — no factory fields, no provider wire types in the orchestration path, verdicts engine-validated from Result.StructuredJSON — so the nil-factory SIGSEGV class is unrepresentable and a toolless dispatch cannot reach a loop role.
rationale: The central rewire. Grounding against live code: today RunSlice dispatches via RunSliceOptions.NewAgent/NewVerifier factory fields with nil-defaults added by the 2026-06-28 eval supervisor fix (internal/run/slice.go:57-63,193-201); the verifier is constructed as a full agent then verify.RunAgentic type-asserts model.StructuredOutput and makes ONE zero-tool ChatStructured call (internal/verify/verify.go:189-229 — the sworn#55 gap); implement.Run (internal/implement/implement.go:39) is the sole agent.Run consumer. After this slice: prompt assembly stays orchestrator-side (SystemPrompt/Payload in DispatchInput); the loop execution lives behind the driver; RunAgentic's transport is replaced by the driver dispatch while its verdict-acceptance semantics (baton.ValidateSchema('verifier-verdict-v1'), fail-closed INCONCLUSIVE, terminal-error BLOCKED) are preserved verbatim. RunFirstPass (deterministic $0 pre-flight) is untouched. Telemetry legs (appendDispatch at slice.go:125 call sites) switch their source to Result fields; full cost honesty lands in S08.
in_scope: RunSlice captain/implement/verify legs via Registry.Resolve + Driver.Dispatch
Deleting the NewAgent/NewVerifier factory fields from RunSliceOptions
Verdict acceptance relocated to Result.StructuredJSON with acceptStructuredVerdict semantics preserved verbatim
Import-boundary test over internal/run, internal/verify, internal/scheduler
implement.go prompt-assembly seam (prompts orchestrator-side, loop driver-side)
out_of_scope: Telemetry honesty (S08 — this slice only pins Result as the plumbing source)

effort_complexity.rationale: The largest seam rewire in the release: three role legs, verify.go transport replacement with semantics preservation, factory deletion, and an import-boundary contract — regression risk concentrated here.

risks.description: Verdict-acceptance semantics drift during the transport swap (fail-closed INCONCLUSIVE / terminal BLOCKED mappings are load-bearing for the slice state machine).
risks.mitigation: The existing verify_test.go acceptance/validation tests are kept and adapted, not rewritten; acceptStructuredVerdict and baton.ValidateSchema remain the single acceptance path; any behaviour change must fail an existing test first.

risks.description: A tightened dispatch seam regresses test fixtures in OTHER packages (the S05-strict-reader lesson: internal/board fixtures broke across packages).
risks.mitigation: The slice's test commands include the affected packages, and the track merge gate runs the full `go test ./...` — the memory-documented failure mode is named in the journal template for this slice.

risks.description: AC-03's 'terminal error kinds -> BLOCKED' preserves acceptStructuredVerdict's existing semantics verbatim, but today's terminal check (internal/run/slice.go:487, model.IsTerminal on the implement leg's returned *model.Error, KindAuth/KindCredits) reads model.ErrorKind, not the new internal/driver.Result.ErrKind string vocabulary S02 introduces. Swapping the transport without an explicit mapping silently drops the fail-fast-on-auth property (flagged during S02's Captain design review, 2026-07-03; S02 spec.json R-03 is the producing side of this contract).
risks.mitigation: This slice's implementation must explicitly treat Result.ErrKind IN {"auth", "credits"} (from any driver, not just claude-cli) as terminal at both consumption points: the implement leg (replacing the slice.go:487 model.IsTerminal check) and the verify leg's acceptStructuredVerdict 'terminal error kinds -> BLOCKED' mapping in AC-03. The set is {auth, credits}, not auth alone — the S04 Coach acknowledgement (T3 captain-proceed.md, 2026-07-10) is the binding record: subprocess drivers collapse all terminal cases to auth, but the in-process driver emits credits as its own kind, and an auth-only check silently loses the credits fail-fast. Add tests asserting BOTH an ErrKind=auth AND an ErrKind=credits Dispatch result halt immediately rather than entering the retry/escalation path.

risks.description: Advertised-vs-actual routing honesty (S05 Captain escalate pin, Coach-ratified 2026-07-10): S05's `sworn capabilities` enumeration advertises ViaProxy for in-process prefixes keyed on the same login condition model.FromEnv uses, but the registry's resolved in-process drivers construct their client via proxy-blind model.NewClient — so after this slice rewires the loop onto registry dispatch, execution would go direct while the capability surface claims proxy routing, regressing the keyless-credits (S06b/OpenRouter) journey.
risks.mitigation: BINDING for this slice: registry-dispatched in-process drivers must obtain their model client through the FromEnv-equivalent resolution path (or an extracted shared helper) so the actual dispatch route satisfies the exact condition `sworn capabilities` advertises. Add a test asserting that under the proxy login condition a registry-resolved in-process driver's dispatch goes via the proxy route, and that the capabilities enumeration and the dispatch condition evaluate from the same predicate (no second hand-synced table). The track merges as one unit, so the S05-landed/S06-pending intermediate state never reaches the integration branch.

acceptance_criteria.text: RunSliceOptions SHALL NOT expose NewAgent/NewVerifier factory fields (deleted, not deprecated); every role leg (captain, implement, verify) SHALL obtain its driver via Registry.Resolve(modelID, role) and dispatch via Driver.Dispatch; tests inject fake drivers through a test registry, not through factories.

If Resolve fails for the implement or verify role leg, RunSlice SHALL return a descriptive error naming the model, role, and registered alternatives BEFORE any model dispatch — no nil dereference is possible because no code path holds an unresolved driver (TestRunSliceResolutionFailure). If Resolve fails for the captain leg, RunSlice SHALL record that same descriptive role error as a durable Rule 2 deferral via the existing design-gate deferral path (recordDesignGateDeferral, sworn#51) and proceed — a hard error here would halt every subprocess-first run, since no subprocess driver declares RoleCaptain (Coach decision 2026-07-10, S06 captain-proceed.md pin 1; follow-up to declare RoleCaptain on subprocess drivers tracked separately).

When the verify leg's Dispatch returns, the engine SHALL validate Result.StructuredJSON against verifier-verdict-v1 via the existing acceptStructuredVerdict + baton.ValidateSchema path with semantics preserved verbatim: invalid or missing StructuredJSON -> INCONCLUSIVE (fail-closed), terminal error kinds -> BLOCKED, valid verdict -> mapped Result; the existing verify acceptance tests SHALL pass against the new transport.

internal/run, internal/verify, and internal/scheduler SHALL NOT import internal/model wire types (ChatMessage, ToolDef, ChatResponse, ToolCall) — enforced by TestNoWireImports in internal/run/imports_test.go covering all three packages and failing with the offending package + import named.

Every appendDispatch telemetry call SHALL source duration/tokens/cost/confirmed-model-id from the Dispatch leg's Result fields (honest population semantics land in S08; this AC pins the plumbing source); RunFirstPass SHALL remain byte-identical in behaviour (its tests unchanged); `go test ./internal/run/... ./internal/verify/... ./internal/implement/...` SHALL pass.
